name: Open-data export for academies — lock-in via tooling, not data hoarding
thesis: Academies adopt the candidate's drill and tag taxonomy; staff training compounds the cost of switching tools without the candidate hoarding the underlying data.
experience_sketch: Academy adopts the tagging schema across coaches.
New coach hires train against the schema.
Performance reviews cite the schema directly.
Academy feels the schema is the standard.
Switching to a competitor demands re-training every coach.

defensibility.reasoning: Learning curve becomes the lock-in; data exports remain free, so the candidate cannot be accused of vendor capture.

evidence.quant_signal: Padel United (https://www.padel-united.com/about) and similar club groups document the staffing scale that benefits from a single schema.

risks.counter_mechanic: A club federation publishes a competing free schema.

kill_experiment.description: Pilot the schema with a single multi-court academy; measure coach onboarding time before vs after.
kill_experiment.success_threshold: New-coach onboarding cut by 30 percent within four weeks.

dependencies.stack_fit: Schema definition document; cross-coach training assets.
